name: Silkstead
coordsSource: web-verified:savills(hamlet SW of Winchester nr Hursley/Compton);rightmove-SO21-2LG; cross-checked listing-centroid (n=15, ~120m); 2026-06-04
overview: Silkstead is a small rural hamlet partly within Hursley civil parish in the City of Winchester district of Hampshire, reached by a narrow lane off the Hursley road via Bunstead, south-west of Winchester and roughly 3-4 miles from the city. It is a scattered cluster of houses and farms (Silkstead Lane, Silkstead Farm) in wooded chalk countryside between Hursley and Compton/Otterbourne. The hamlet has no amenities of its own; the nearest village facilities are in Hursley, with full shops, supermarkets and a mainline station in Winchester.
character: A secluded, leafy hamlet of farms and country houses along quiet lanes amid woodland and downland on Winchester's south-western edge. The atmosphere is private and rural, with walking and riding routes (including Yew Hill and the Hursley lanes) close by.
amenities: No shop or pub in Silkstead itself
All Saints Church, primary school and pubs in Hursley (~2 mi)
Otterbourne and Compton village shops nearby
Full shopping, supermarkets and services in Winchester (~3-4 mi)
Chandler's Ford retail and rail (~3 mi)

schools.name: John Keble CofE Primary School, Hursley

commutes.notes: Via Hursley/Otterbourne lanes and the A3090; mainline station
From Winchester station (SWR), drive ~12 min to the station

prices.summary: Silkstead and the Hursley fringe are a premium location near Winchester; housing is mostly substantial detached and period country houses, frequently £700k-£2m+, with very limited supply and strong demand.

thingsToDo: Walking Yew Hill and the Hursley/Silkstead lanes
Farley Mount Country Park (a short drive)
Hursley village and All Saints Church
Winchester's cathedral, culture and shops nearby
placesToEat: The King's Head and The Dolphin in Hursley (~2 mi)
Wide choice of restaurants in Winchester (~3-4 mi)
pros: Very private, wooded rural setting close to Winchester
Good village primary at Hursley and city schools within reach
Quick drive to Winchester's mainline and amenities
Excellent walking and country park nearby
cons: No amenities in the hamlet; car essential
Very expensive and scarce housing stock
whoItSuits: Buyers seeking a secluded, high-value country hamlet on Winchester's doorstep, with good schooling at Hursley and a short commute to the city, who can match the premium market.

sources.label: Ofsted - John Keble CofE Primary School
AllTrails - Hursley, Yew Hill and Silkstead Lane
